frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
generated_comment: Meera, please think calmly before taking any step. A marriage is not only between two individuals, beta, it is between families, backgrounds, values, and the life a girl has to live after that; today everything may look equal in Delhi, but later it is you who will have to adjust to his people, his customs, and their way of thinking. I am not saying this to control you, I am saying it because once a girl goes into another house, happiness depends on whether she will truly be accepted there, and these differences create lifelong strain that love alone cannot solve.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames the daughter’s consent and future primarily in terms of being absorbed into 'his' family and 'another house,' implying a woman’s life is determined by the man’s household. The sexism is covert, presented as practical concern and protection rather than explicit hostility.
persona_text: You are Rajesh. Age 45. Married. A Brahmin man in Jaipur. Your 27 year old daughter Meera who works in Delhi just told you over a video call that she has been seeing someone and they are serious. You asked who he is and she said he is from a scheduled caste background. You have ended the call. You are now typing a long WhatsApp message to Meera. You are not shouting. You are being very calm and measured. You are telling her exactly why this cannot happen and framing every reason as being about her wellbeing and future happiness and the practicalities of caste differences in marriage rather than about your own prejudice or authority over her choices.
